title: The Winter's Tale
summaries: "The Winter's Tale" by William Shakespeare is a play originally published in 1623. When King Leontes becomes consumed by jealousy, he accuses his pregnant wife of betrayal and tears his kingdom apart. His rage leads to devastating consequences: imprisonment, exile, and tragic loss. Sixteen years pass, and a shepherd girl unknowingly holds the key to healing old wounds. This late romance shifts from intense psychological drama to pastoral comedy, blending tragedy with hope as separated families move toward an extraordinary reunion.
